$schema_notes.purpose: Canonical building specification for the spire (la flèche) of Notre-Dame de Paris — Viollet-le-Duc's 1859 flèche, reconstructed à l'identique (96 m) after the 15 Apr 2019 fire. Building #2 proving the Yingzao pipeline rerun. Every dimensional node carries a provenance class + a real source URL + a rights verdict. The Rule Engine derives geometry ONLY from values in this file plus the Gothic ruleset; nothing renders without a source. SPIRE ONLY for v1 — cathedral / nave / la forêt are the documented post-v1 roadmap.
$schema_notes.data_source: Every number traces to docs/NOTRE_DAME_VERIFIED_CORPUS.md (ND-1 output: 16 adversarially-verified verdicts, 40 explicit gaps). NEVER sourced from the fabricated Reims/Nohesive docs.

provenance_classes.measured: Published survey/instrument value or a multiply-confirmed published dimension (factual, uncopyrightable). Requires >=2 independent sources to be 'measured'.
provenance_classes.reconstructed_design: 19th-c. design intent reconstructed from Viollet-le-Duc / Lassus public-domain drawings (the flèche is NOT medieval fabric — it is an 1859 design).
provenance_classes.rule_derived: Derived by OUR engine from the Gothic ruleset (ad quadratum / ad triangulum, Roriczer pinnacle rule, two-centred arch). Must cite the rule.
provenance_classes.conjecture: Flagged uncertain: contested estimate, photo-deduced, scaled off a drawing where no number is published, or lost fabric. Renders in conjecture color; MUST NOT be presented as measured.
provenance_classes.GAP: Could not source. NEVER filled with a number. The corpus ships 40 of these as the model — emulate, never guess.

units.module: pied du roi (royal foot) = 324.8 mm — the French-Gothic metrological anchor, the cai-fen analog. The derivation log shows the module math in pieds; emitted component positions/dimensions are in METRES so the verifier reads 96 m / 30 m / the octagon footprint directly.

source_registry._note: The rights-cleared registry. The G09 source-registry check (verify.mjs) HARD-FAILS any component whose `source` key is not present here with a `use` or `use-data` verdict. CITE-only and NEITHER sources may be referenced in prose but may NEVER back a rendered component value. This is the DQ-avoidance gate: unauthorized assets = disqualification.

VLD-FLECHE.citation: Viollet-le-Duc, Dictionnaire raisonné de l'architecture française, art. 'Flèche', t.5 — 'Élévation et plans de la flèche', engraving.
VLD-FLECHE.reason: Author d.1879 → PD worldwide. THE measured-drawing asset (elevation + plans). Cite BnF for provenance. Scaled dimensions off the plate are rule_derived/reconstructed_design.

VLD-DICT.citation: Viollet-le-Duc, Dictionnaire raisonné t.5 'Flèche' text + figures (Wikisource).
VLD-DICT.reason: PD (d.1879). Structural/charpente system figures and the six-part profile description.

CULTURE-FLECHE.citation: Ministère de la Culture — site officiel Notre-Dame de Paris, 'Petite histoire de la flèche'.
CULTURE-FLECHE.reason: Dimensions are uncopyrightable facts → ingest numbers (96 m, 30/33 m, profile, ornament). Do not ingest page prose/images.

FRIENDS.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — official cathedral facts / spire page.
FRIENDS.reason: Confirmed dimensional / weight set (500 t oak, 250 t lead). Numbers are facts; prose/images are not ingested.

NDP-SPIRE.citation: Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris (official) — 'The spire' / architecture pages.
NDP-SPIRE.reason: Official confirmation of 96 m and the ~30 m crossing anchor. Numbers only.

CONSTRUCTIONBTP.citation: constructionbtp.com — 'Flèche Notre-Dame de Paris, chef-d'œuvre de charpente en chêne' (2023).
CONSTRUCTIONBTP.reason: Base anchored 30 m above the crossing; ornament counts (crockets, pinnacles, gargoyles, quadrilobes); reconstruction à l'identique figures. Facts only.

RESTAURONS-ND.citation: Restaurons Notre-Dame — 'le tabouret ou la souche de la flèche de Viollet-le-Duc'.
RESTAURONS-ND.reason: Tabouret 15×13 m trapezoid footprint, 80 t / 110 pieces (medium confidence — flagged in notes). Facts only.

WIKIPEDIA-SPIRE.citation: Wikipedia — 'Spire of Notre-Dame de Paris' / 'Flèche de Notre-Dame de Paris'.
WIKIPEDIA-SPIRE.reason: Multi-source confirmation of 96 m, statuary programme, rooster, fire timeline. Cross-check only; numbers are facts.

PERSEE-2009.citation: Bulletin Monumental 2009, t.167-2 (Persée) — Viollet-le-Duc spire statuary.
PERSEE-2009.reason: 16 copper statues — 12 apostles (4 groups of 3) + 4 evangelist symbols; heights/weights; St Thomas with VLD's features. Facts only.

MEDIACHIMIE.citation: mediachimie.org — the copper statuary of the spire.
MEDIACHIMIE.reason: Corroborates apostle 3.40 m / 140 kg, evangelist 2.0 m / 65 kg copper figures. Facts only.

RORICZER1486.citation: Mathias Roriczer, 'Büchlein von der Fialen Gerechtigkeit' (1486); 1847 PD translation, Archaeological Journal v.4.
RORICZER1486.reason: The pinnacle rule: base square → 45°-rotated inscribed squares (side ratio 1/√2). 6×/7× vertical multiples are CITED-UNCERTAIN → any value using them is conjecture, never measured.

AD-QUADRATUM.citation: Ad quadratum: successive square side ratio 1/√2 ≈ 0.7071 (Salama et al. 2016; pure Euclid).
AD-QUADRATUM.reason: Pure geometry — uncopyrightable construction rule. Drives the octagon-from-square and pinnacle insets.

AD-TRIANGULUM.citation: Ad triangulum: triangle height = base × √3/2 ≈ 0.8660 (Hiscock, 'The Symbol at Your Door', ch.10; pure geometry).
AD-TRIANGULUM.reason: Pure geometry — sets elevation heights from plan width. Used only where no measured/scaled value exists; never overrides a sourced value (V08).

FRIENDS-LAYOUT.citation: Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris — 'The Majestic Layout of Notre-Dame' (architecture/layout page).
FRIENDS-LAYOUT.reason: Official confirmed headline geometry for the WHOLE cathedral: length 128 m, width 48 m at the transept, west facade 43.5 m / 45 m, nave vessel 13 m, side aisle 5.9 m, 10 nave bays, vault 33 m, height under roof 43 m, transept 48 x 14 m. Dimensions are uncopyrightable facts → ingest numbers; prose/images are not ingested.

TECHNO-SCIENCE.citation: techno-science.net — 'Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris', éléments architecturaux extérieurs / structure et dimensions (mirror of FR Wikipedia article text).
TECHNO-SCIENCE.reason: Independent corroboration of the west facade ('43,5 mètres / 135 pieds-du-roi' wide, '45 mètres' high) and the central vessel ('largeur du vaisseau central de la nef : 13 mètres'). Numbers are facts; prose is not ingested.

WIKI-NDP.reason: Confirmed west tower height 69 m and nave vault 32.5 m; cross-check of the headline dimensional set. Numbers are facts; prose/images are not ingested.

DEHIO-BEZOLD.citation: Dehio & von Bezold, 'Die kirchliche Baukunst des Abendlandes', atlas (UB Heidelberg, Public Domain Mark 1.0) — measured plans/sections of Notre-Dame de Paris.
DEHIO-BEZOLD.reason: Explicit Public Domain Mark — the cleanest open repository for Notre-Dame plans/sections. Scaled dimensions off the plates are reconstructed_design; used here for the longitudinal massing layout (bay rhythm, nave-vs-choir division) as a published plan reference where the corpus does not publish an exact split.

rights_excluded._note: Restricted sources — CITE-ONLY or NEITHER. These may NEVER back a rendered component (G09 rejects them). Listed so the registry is auditable and so a value mis-attributed to them hard-fails (V13).

TALLON-SCAN.reason: Andrew Tallon ~1B-pt laser cloud — no open license, custody unclear. Facts citable; data not ingestible.

AGP-SCAN.reason: AGP ~50B-pt surveys — proprietary commissioned deliverable, no reuse license.

CNRS-TWIN.reason: CNRS/De Luca digital twin / Aioli — restricted, authenticated access, no public CC license.

CHARTRES-BLOG.reason: chartrescathedralconceptualplan.wordpress.com — self-published; amateur ad-quadratum derivations MIS-attributed to Bork/Tallon. Hard DQ — never ingest, never cite as measured.

meta.name_fr: Flèche de la cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris

date.value: spire designed/erected 1859 (Viollet-le-Duc); lost in the fire 15 Apr 2019; rebuilt à l'identique, cathedral reopened 7–8 Dec 2024; new spire summit + rooster 2023

meta.build_state: Reconstructed à l'identique — the same Viollet-le-Duc design, 96 m, rebuilt 2021–2024 in oak + lead with the original statuary (removed 11 Apr 2019, four days before the fire, so the apostles/evangelists survive).

fire_event.value: ignition in la forêt ~18:18; flèche collapse ~19:50 on 15 Apr 2019; ~750 t of stone and lead fell

pied_du_roi_mm.note: Royal foot = 0.3248 m. The unit, like Nanchan's chi, is the metrological anchor for the derivation log. Standard French historical metrology.

ad_quadratum.value: successive square side ratio = 1/√2 ≈ 0.70711; the octagon springs from a square by 45° rotation; area halves between insets

ad_triangulum.value: triangle height = base × √3/2 ≈ 0.86603 — sets elevation height from plan width where no measured/scaled value exists

roriczer_pinnacle.value: base square → 45°-rotated inscribed squares (side ratio 1/√2): outer = plinth, next = shaft, inner = panel. Auszug (spirelet) to vertical. Vertical multiples (shaft ~6×, spire ~7× base width) are CITED-UNCERTAIN.
roriczer_pinnacle.note: Used for the spire's pinnacles. 6×/7× multiples flagged conjecture — never presented as exact.

total_height.note: To summit / rooster head, above street level. 'Culminant à 96 mètres.' Multiply confirmed (>=2 independent sources) → measured. VERIFIER ANCHOR V08.

base_level.note: Anchored ~30 m above the ground, above the croisée du transept (crossing). One Culture source gives 33 m for the tabouret platform — accept 30–33 m. Enrayure zéro ~1 m above the crossing vaults. VERIFIER ANCHOR V08.

height_above_base.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN: DERIVED 96 − 30. 93 m (rooster base) vs 96 m (rooster head) ambiguity; ~45 m commonly rendered above the ridge. Not a single clean published figure — see _GAP_per_section_heights.

base_footprint.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN (single medium-confidence source → not measured per the >=2-source guardrail). Irregular crossing footprint (tabouret). Used only as a CROSS-CHECK that the octagon inscribes within the ~14 m crossing; the octagon's actual proportions come from the VLD-FLECHE plate (reconstructed_design), not from this number. The exact octagon springing dimension is a GAP.

profile_six_parts.value: 1 tabouret → 2 souche → 3 octagonal fût (with quadrilobes) → 4 first openwork gallery → 5 second level (8 gabled bays) → 6 aiguille + coq; octagon angles set on the roof ridges + 4 valleys
profile_six_parts.note: THE taper schema — drives the rule engine's spire profile. Six parts confirmed; their individual heights are NOT published (see _GAP_per_section_heights) → scaled off the VLD plate proportions, tagged rule_derived.

octagon_faces.note: Octagonal plan, 8 faces, with the 4 cardinal faces on the roof ridges and the 4 diagonal faces over the valleys. Confirmed by the 'plans' half of the VLD elevation+plans plate.

tabouret.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN (single medium-confidence source → not measured per the >=2-source guardrail). The stool (souche) transferring spire load to the 4 crossing piers. 80 t is PART OF the 500 t timber total, not additional.

oak_framework.note: Oak armature (charpente de chêne). ~1000 pieces. Confirmed multi-source.

lead_cladding.note: Lead sheathing over the oak armature. Confirmed multi-source.

ornament.value: hundreds of crockets (crochets) + floral motifs across the 8 faces; pinnacles; bay arcatures; 8 quadrilobes; gargoyles
ornament.note: REVISED after adversarial verification (artifacts/adversarial-verification.json, research guardrail #2). The '~200 crockets per face / 32 gargoyles' framing was REFUTED — it traces to a single non-independent press cluster, while the official Ministry source (culture.gouv 'Spécial ND 4/6') gives DIFFERENT figures: ~112 crochets gothiques on the aiguille arêtes, 16 gargouilles (not 32), 8 grands-ducs + 8 griffons on the pinnacles. So the ornament COUNTS are conjecture (cited-uncertain). The rendered ornament (8 quadrilobes, 16 pinnacles, gabled bays) takes its FORM from the VLD public-domain plate (reconstructed_design), not from these contested counts.

statues.arrangement: 4 groups of 3 (one apostle group + ... actually 4 corners × [3 apostles staggered]) with the 4 evangelist symbols (angel/Matthew, lion/Mark, ox/Luke, eagle/John) leading each group; staggered up the base of the spire, climbing toward heaven; St Thomas (patron of architects) turns to face the spire and bears Viollet-le-Duc's own features
statues.note: 16 copper statues by Geoffroi-Dechaume. Removed 11 Apr 2019, four days before the fire → originals survive. VERIFIER ANCHOR V10: 16 = 12 + 4; apostle ≈ 3.40 m, evangelist ≈ 2.0 m; St Thomas faces inward. The COUNT, HEIGHTS and ARRANGEMENT are measured + adversarially corroborated; the WEIGHTS are cited-uncertain (apostle 140 vs ~150 kg per culture.gouv/diocèse; evangelist 65 vs ~75 kg) per artifacts/adversarial-verification.json — so weights are recorded as contested ranges, not asserted, and drive no geometry (the statues render at their measured HEIGHTS).

rooster.note: CITED-UNCERTAIN weight: ~30 kg (most sources) vs ~10 kg (one ND culture page) — the two sources CONFLICT, so the weight is conjecture (V13 requires rooster weight <= conjecture). The coq's existence and ~96 m summit POSITION are measured (from total_height); only this published mass is uncertain. Gilded copper coq holds 3 relics; new rooster (Olivier Brisville) installed 16 Dec 2023.

_GAP_per_section_heights.note: DO NOT FILL with invented numbers. Exact heights of the souche / octagonal fût / each openwork gallery / aiguille are NOT published. The engine scales them off the VLD 'Flèche' plate (BnF ark mm320202712p) proportions and tags every per-section height rule_derived ('scaled from PD plate, ratio × 66 m visible extent'), or leaves them conjecture. The 96 m total and 30 m base are the only hard measured anchors.

_GAP_octagon_springing.note: DO NOT FILL. The precise octagon springing dimension at the spire base is not published as a number. The engine inscribes a regular octagon in the mean of the 15×13 m tabouret trapezoid (rule_derived); the exact dimension stays a gap.

_GAP_above_ridge_height.note: DO NOT FILL. No single authoritative 'height above roof' figure. 96 m above ground and ~30 m base are sourced; ~66 m above-base and ~45 m visible-above-ridge are derived/approximate. Sources disagree 93 m (rooster base) vs 96 m (rooster head).

cathedral_massing._note: PASS 1 WHOLE-CATHEDRAL MASSING. Headline dimensions are the adversarially-verified cathedral_geometry from docs/NOTRE_DAME_VERIFIED_CORPUS.md (each is `measured`, multiply-sourced → carries corroborating_sources so comp()'s >=2-source gate passes). Where the corpus does NOT publish a split or placement (the crossing position along the 128 m, the nave-vs-choir division, the individual bay interval, the wall depths, the tower footprints, the aisle/roof heights) the value is `rule_derived` (or a GAP rendered as a plain massing volume), tagged with a note — never asserted as measured. The spire (data/notre-dame-canonical.json `spire`) stays FULL FIDELITY and is composed in at the crossing UNCHANGED; the massing is partial fidelity by design (the honest fidelity gradient). World frame: crossing at the origin, +X toward the chevet, -X toward the west front, +-Z the transept arms, +Y up; all values in metres.

total_length.note: = 420 ft. Total exterior length, west front to chevet. CONFIRMED (FRIENDS + Wikipedia infobox). The verifier recomputes this from the extreme component span on X (V-LEN); the internal split along the 128 m is rule_derived (crossing position is not published).

total_width_transept.note: Maximum building width = transept arm-to-arm length = chevet outer diameter. CONFIRMED (FRIENDS layout; corroborated Le Marais Mood). North arm tip ~+24 m, south arm tip ~-24 m about the crossing origin.

transept_arm_width.note: Width of the transept arm (the narrowly-defined 'transept width'), distinct from the 48 m arm-to-arm length. Sets the crossing footprint along X and the transept arm thickness.

west_facade_width.note: West facade width. CONFIRMED (FRIENDS; independently techno-science.net '43,5 mètres / 135 pieds-du-roi'). Spans the two towers + the three portals across +-Z at the -X end.

facade_height_no_towers.note: Height of the west facade screen excluding the towers (to the top of the Gallery of Kings gable zone). CONFIRMED (FRIENDS; techno-science.net '45 mètres / 141 pieds').

west_tower_height.note: Height of each west tower. CONFIRMED (de.Wikipedia + FRIENDS). The dropped '297 steps' claim was REFUTED — not used. Tower footprint/spacing along Z is NOT published → rule_derived.

nave_vessel_width.note: Clear width of the central vessel (vaisseau central). CONFIRMED (FRIENDS; techno-science.net 'vaisseau central 13 m'; de.Wikipedia 'über 12 Metern'). Same vessel width is carried through nave AND choir.

side_aisle_width.note: Width of each inner side aisle. CONFIRMED (FRIENDS layout, corroborated by official notredamedeparis.fr / techno-science). Chevet/ambulatory aisle laser-measured 5.76 m (Bork/Tallon) is a CITE-only cross-check, not used to render.

nave_bays.note: 10 nave bays, paired into 5 sexpartite double-bays. COUNT is measured; the individual bay INTERVAL (~6 m) is rule_derived (a directly-published single-bay dimension was not located — corpus gap).

vault_crown_height.note: High-vault crown (keystone) height above floor, nave AND choir. CONFIRMED (FRIENDS 33 m = 108 ft; de.Wikipedia 32.5 m; PARISCityVISION 33 m). MEASURED-REALITY GUARD: must NEVER be recomputed toward an ad-quadratum/ad-triangulum ideal, and is NOT 35 m (refuted 'general interior height') nor 43 m (height under roof).

height_under_roof.note: Height under the nave roof (hauteur sous toit, 141 ft). CONFIRMED; CORRECTED from the refuted ~35 m claim. The massing nave/choir vessel rises to the 33 m crown; the steep roof ridge sits near this 43 m level.

chevet_outer_diameter.note: Chevet (apse) outer diameter ~47.88 m, equal to the 48 m maximum width. The apse hemicycle massing radius derives from this. The Bork/Tallon CONCENTRIC chevet radii (6.65/12.42/18.18/23.94 m) trace only to a self-published blog (CHARTRES-BLOG, rights_excluded) → GAP, never rendered as measured; the apse is a plain massing hemicycle (rule_derived form).

_placement_crossing_position.note: RULE-DERIVED, not published. The corpus gives total length 128 m but does NOT publish where the transept crossing falls along it, nor a clean modern nave-vs-choir interior split (Bell's 1902 PD nave 225 ft ~68.6 m is a different/interior reference, cross-check only). The composer places the crossing at the world origin and derives the west-front / nave / choir X-extents from {128 m total, 14 m crossing footprint, 48 m chevet diameter, ~6 m bay interval}; the split is rule_derived and flagged, never asserted as measured. Uncertainty propagated to PLACEMENT.

_placement_bay_interval.note: RULE-DERIVED bay interval. The nave runs 10 measured bays over the rule_derived nave length; the per-bay interval (~4.9-6 m) is not a directly-published single-bay survey value (corpus gap) — taken off the Dehio-Bezold PD plan rhythm. The COUNT (10) is measured; the INTERVAL is rule_derived.

_GAP_aisle_vault_heights.note: DO NOT FILL. Inner/outer aisle + tribune vault crown heights are not published numerically (corpus gap). The massing aisle volumes rise to a rule_derived height below the 33 m vessel crown; this is rendered as a plain rule_derived block, never as a measured aisle vault height.

_GAP_wall_depths.note: DO NOT FILL. The west-front wall depth, tower footprint dimensions and tower spacing along Z are not published (corpus gap). The massing uses rule_derived block depths/footprints sized to read correctly; never asserted as measured.

verifier_targets._note: Machine-checkable assertions, recomputed by verify.mjs FROM component coordinates (never from key_dimensions). Scene length unit = metre; tolerances explicit. Spire-only subset of the corpus V01–V14 (cathedral/nave/la forêt checks are post-v1).

checks.assert: Spire total height = 96 m, base anchored at ~30 m (accept 30–33), visible extent ≈ 66 m. MEASURED-REALITY GUARD: the engine must NOT normalize the spire toward any ad-triangulum/Roriczer ideal that contradicts the sourced 96 m / 30 m — a spire 'corrected' away from 96 m total or 30 m base is a CRITICAL failure.
Spire is octagonal (8 faces) on the ~14 m mean crossing footprint, with the six-part profile present (tabouret, souche, fût, 2 openwork galleries, aiguille+coq).
Spire statuary: 16 copper statues = 12 apostles (4 groups of 3, staggered heights) + 4 evangelist symbols; apostle ≈ 3.40 m, evangelist ≈ 2.0 m; St Thomas faces inward.
Taper monotonic: cross-section width strictly decreases from souche to aiguille (no bulge); rooster (coq) is the single topmost component at ~96 m.

Provenance audit: every component carries a valid class + a non-empty source that resolves to the rights-cleared source_registry with a use/use-data verdict; 0 unsourced; the per-section heights / octagon springing render rule_derived or conjecture, never measured.

No-invention guard: no component may carry a `measured` class while citing a restricted source (Tallon/AGP/CNRS/chartres-blog) or a GAP value; the rooster weight (cited-uncertain) and per-section heights stay <= rule_derived/conjecture.
Rights gate (G09): every component `source` is in source_registry with verdict use/use-data; a source resolving to rights_excluded or absent from the registry FAILS the build.
Each view is non-blank (>3% non-background pixels).
Provenance view shows all four evidence-class colors.

ideal_deviations_index._note: The provenance layer's exhibit list — where the real flèche disagrees with a generic Gothic ideal. Pipeline rule: measured reality overrides the rulebook; deviations are annotated, never 'fixed' (V08).
items: Spire total 96 m and base 30 m are MEASURED — never recomputed from an ad-triangulum height-from-width ideal (V08 critical).
Per-section heights are NOT published → scaled off the PD plate (rule_derived), never asserted as measured.
Octagon springing dimension is a GAP → a regular octagon is inscribed in the mean ~14 m footprint (rule_derived), never claimed as surveyed.
Rooster weight is cited-uncertain (~30 vs ~10 kg) → conjecture, never measured.
Roriczer 6×/7× pinnacle multiples are cited-uncertain → pinnacle heights are conjecture, never exact.
